description: Practical, technical, learning and process Problems
author: Nanette B.
pros: It`s very uncomplicated to get the answers to questions about everything I work and deal with.
cons: Maybe it will be easier, when the the software will be integrated to another app?
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Constantino W.
pros: I really like the ease of use and the value of sharing information on this way with my colleagues.
cons: There are some questions who comes up again and again although they’ve been answered already. I think this can be fixed with an improved GUI

description: I am engaged in providing technical solution to queries posted on Starmind. But reading response of non technical questions is also a great source of knowledge management.
author: Roshni C.
pros: User friendly, the dash board, robust search
cons: the layout. I think the previous layout was better (may be a question of a habit) but for me the navigation bar ''Ask the ICRC'' on right hand side. Also would it be possible to put the dashboard on the top (a source of motivation how am I doing!!!) than at the bottom. Also ''Notifications' should be on Top that way its more catchy!

review_title: Great way of connecting employees cross geographical, functional and cultural borders
author: Caroline G.
pros: I appreciate the easy-to use interface and the content understanding functionality that helps me identify if similar questions have been asked
cons: It would have been powerful to being able to connect to other databases and search in documents for the information

review_title: With Starmind hear from your Organization experts
description: getting expert into one umbrella was the biggest challenge which has been solved.
author: Ashish G.
pros: Expert picking from the related queries helps end user to get an answer which is not possible via ticket or time consuming.
cons: Finding an answer for old question posted. Search can be enhanced.

description: overall it is a really good experience. Particularly valuable fo rnewcomers who do not know who to ask / where to find informations and/or are afraid to ask questions.
author: Julien S.
pros: Like the team spirit coming from this collaborative way of working. I also like the challenge/ranking etc -> it becomes fun to help the others !
cons: Quality of the replies is not always perfect. Needs some distance/critical thinking sometimes. The good thing is that incorrect replies will receive bad rating from other users.

author: Luca C.
pros: It connects people easily, adds value to employee interaction
cons: UI a bit confusing, recently asked questions should pop up more easily

review_title: Best online forum for water cooler discussions
description: Found a lot of opportunities and interesting collaboration opportunities
author: Louie M.
pros: Machine learning is great to find experts by topic. Replying to old questions should be discouraged using UX nudges
cons: More iconography and push features... Better semantic indexing visualizations

review_title: Fun way to keep everyone informed
author: Jonathan C.
pros: I love the way employees can help each other to solve common problems
cons: Sometimes it takes a lot of digging to get the right topic

description: Working in Support I can avoid a lot of tickets if I answer users questions there.
author: Nicolas H.
pros: Quick and easy source for question and answers within the company
cons: Used sometimes as knowledge base by administrators and those entries often come on top which easy indentify them as "created" questions.

review_title: Helps identify weak distribution of information
author: Juliana S.
pros: Following questions, it helps see how the answers evolve or become resolved. Additionally the skill map is a great way to see the user's knowledge base.
cons: Tagging relevant users to a question. Sometimes I see questions that don't include significant users to answer, even though the follow tags and sometimes have the question subject in their title.

description: Starmind has helped me several times find relevant content without having to ask anyone else but starmind. I am very impressed by Starmind capabilities.
author: Ulysse M.
pros: Info is easy to find using the search bar at the top and suggested exisitng content answering our question
cons: I believe that sometimes the community does not answer exactly the question asked but then it might also come from the non-clarity of some questions asked.

description: overall working good and easy to use.
author: keivan M.
pros: its fast and interface is good and easy to read
cons: the browsing of posts is a bit messy, the date submitted and such should be in the same line
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Tomáš B.
pros: I like the possibilities of people sharing their knowledge and wisdom freely and easily.
cons: The software is still a bit "young" hence the amount of active users is low, but it will surely rise!

description: I am attracted to Starmind because I love to share knowledge and support people. It is also my place to go to search for simply questions and answers. There is a source of information in there that if used properly it become a good platform to visit everyday.
author: Julie S.
pros: I like that people can write an anonymous question without being exposed. This gives them the opportunity to be open and honest. The people who write the answers however are known and can show their knowledge.
cons: No sure I found any that this point. I do like the software.

review_title: An excellent platform to build up / support Crowd Intelligence and Know-How Self-Service Models
author: Torsten S.
pros: Great and easy-to-use platform to ease the lives of our IT Servicedesk guys as well as of our entire user community. Using the Starmind is great fun - and even provides knowledge and efficiency benefits.
cons: As pointed out earlier - sometimes, the "flood" of information tends to be even a bit too much... - Maybe it would be possible to add more obvious options to limit the overall information a little bit - this would make the platform even more valuable, I guess...

review_title: Excellent software to encourage open exchange on different topics in an organization
author: Florian R.
pros: Starmind helps us in an incredible way to encourage colleagues on a global level not to be afraid to ask questions. Through the feature to ask a question and comment on answers to their questions anonymously, colleagues are feeling more ready to ask anything that they are concerned about. Sometimes, staff in organizations are haunted by difficult or sensitive questions which they do not feel comfortable with asking to their manager or other colleagues. With Starmind, we have a safe space now to also get aware of and address those questions. In addition to that core feature of Starmind the design both of the web version and the mobile app are very user-friendly and the search function works splendidly. With hashtags and skill maps, it gets easy to identify colleagues who are experts in specific areas.
cons: For specific sensitive questions, it might be helpful if the question answerer could also remain anonymous, but his department affiliation would be shown. E.g., an John Doe from HR answers a question, but it will only be shown as "Anonymous, HR".

author: Philip G.
pros: - customer support. [SENSITIVE CONTENT] is always very kind and helpful. - Out of the box integrations - simple to use - the ability to precapture questions and answers for software rollouts (with knowledge spaces)
cons: - Search should be better (more like Google Search) - Every user should be able to mark knowledge thats important for their work - Individualiced filters should be possible - Status of Feature requests on canny is not always clear and it takes a lot of time untile someone reacts on them - beeing able to ask questions that are not recognisable as such

description: I really like it and enjoy to be able to contribute with my knowledge.
author: Edda C.
pros: Starmind is very easy to use. Many people can contribute with their knowledge and experience. I am one of the contributors and I am in level 18 at the moment . It is an addictive tool. In my spare time, I search for questions that I can answer. I am very competitive, and work to reach the highest level possible. This is an interactive tool since we can ask any type of questions and we can chat with other colleagues to get the best answer. There are helpful features such as Feeds, experts, dashboard and skill map which are very convenient for the user.
cons: I have seen many unanswered questions since over a year ago, it would be helpful to questions in a different section. A glossary will be helpful and an appendix for easy lookup of a word since there are many technical words used for a specific business unit.

review_title: Excellent tool to tap into the knowledge of any organisation
description: The tool is easy to use and easy to integrate and requires no training. It has some gamification aspects that make using the tool interesting.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The concept is quite simple: users ask questions and by analyzing the answers, the system clusters the employees according to skills. This is very powerful and allows to quickly find answers as well as helpful people.
cons: It is a bit of a challenge to deal with old answers - the answers have an "expiry date", but it would be nice to be able to retire answers automatically.

description: It feels like Google made a baby with Siri.
author: Cesar K.
pros: In a large company like Swisscom it's not easy to quickly find the right person to help you out with a problem. Starmind, which powers Swisscom's Ask-The-Brain tool, makes this really simple. Questions can be submitted anonymously, and then the system automatically matches it up with the people most likely to have the right answer.
cons: It would be nice to see more integrations into other tools that are used on a daily basis, like Teams, Confluence, or Outlook, to avoid having to search in multiple tools. But I don't know if this is a limitation of the Software or if Swisscom just hasn't tackled it yet.

description: helps save time and energy for solving problems and finding answers
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: easy to use good value for money solution-orientend makes sense and is a pleasure to use
cons: could be more attractive on the graphics and images side

description: I found anwers to question for which I otherwise would hava had to open a ticket.
author: Nik G.
pros: Common questions are being answered by people who are responsible for each segment. Anonymous questioning & answering is also a plus.
cons: Adding pictures or videos would be an advantage.

review_title: A handy and great tool complying to what it stands for!
description: I assume it is time consuming for all those who read things they do not really need
author: Cihan K.
pros: easy implementation after only one meeting as Pioneer the idea of knowledge sharing in shortest time is absolutely a must in a big company by this undocumented knowledge will not be lost completely
cons: users trying to get high ranking by providing data that might not always be useful information is sometimes shared without verifying background (by referring to a specific order, unconnected to general use) no idea how to search for specific data- a kind of data base would be great to keep the data documented and available for the future

review_title: Excellent software - Does the best in every possible way
author: Vishwesh U.
pros: Pros - Easy going UI Excellent UX and options Notifications are amazing and the layout is neat
cons: Not Applicable as i do not see any cons as of now
